channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Utah woman receives 100 cancer treatments, living far beyond doctors' expectations
item.description: An 84-year-old Utah woman recently received her 100th cancer treatment, outliving doctors' expectations.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, KRON4

item.title: Richard Chamberlain, TV actor who starred in 'Dr. Kildare,' dies at 90
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Richard Chamberlain, the handsome hero of the 1960s television series "Dr. Kildare" who found a second career as an award-winning "king of the miniseries," has died. He was 90.
item.dc:creator: BOB THOMAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: Planning to visit these national parks? You may need to make a reservation to get in
item.description: While hundreds of parks are free to enter, others use timed entry and other reservation methods to control crowds — but that also means you may need to secure your spot well in advance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iran if it doesn't agree to nuclear deal
item.description: “If they don’t make a deal, there will be bombing and it will be bombing the likes of which they have never seen before,” Trump said in a comment aired Sunday on NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Iran rejects direct negotiations with the US in response to Trump's letter
item.description: Iran's president said Sunday that the Islamic Republic rejected direct negotiations with the United States over its rapidly advancing nuclear program, offering Tehran's first response to a letter President Donald Trump sent to the country's supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's attorney general on Sunday asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters, a request that came hours before President Donald Trump's ally planned the giveaway at an evening rally.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delta Air Lines drops new set of pilot trading cards: How to get yours
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who is Trevor Milton, the startup founder and donor pardoned by Trump
item.description: Milton said Thursday in a post on social platform X that he spoke to Trump over the phone and that he signed his pardon of “innocence.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Deputies won't face charges in deadly 2022 shootout on California highway, prosecutors say
item.description: San Bernardino County sheriff's deputies involved in a 2022 shootout along a California freeway that killed a teenage girl and her fugitive father will not face any criminal charges, state Attorney General Rob Bonta announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Madison Booker and top-seeded Texas outlast SEC rival Tennessee 67-59 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Texas coach Vic Schaefer got the tense battle he expected between his defensive-minded Longhorns and the highest-scoring team in the country in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: White House may change how journalists can ask Trump questions
item.description: The White House is currently locked in a legal battle with the Associated Press over its decision to ban the wire service from key West Wing spaces over its “Gulf of America” stylebook policy.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KTLA 5

item.title: Deputies won't face charges in deadly 2022 shootout on California highway, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors in California say San Bernardino County deputies involved in a shootout that killed a teenage girl and her fugitive father will not face any criminal charges.
item.dc:creator: Susan Montoya Bryan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: RECALL: Thousands of pounds of Our Family coffee mislabeled
item.description: The FDA lists this as a class II recall, in which use or exposure to a product “may cause temporary or medically reversible adverse health consequences or where the probability of serious adverse health consequences is remote.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Plane crash near Minneapolis sends home up in flames with no survivors on board
item.description: Dramatic video captured the aftermath of a home being engulfed in flames near Minneapolis after a small plane crashed into the home on Saturday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Steph Curry scores a 3-book deal, starting with a 'personal reflection' on his life and career
item.description: The NBA superstar has agreed to release three books through One World, beginning Sept. 9 with “Shot Ready," which Curry is calling a “personal reflection” on his life on and off the court. The book also includes more than 100 photographs.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Death of 90s sitcom actress being investigated by homicide detectives: report
item.description: Per TMZ, the woman has been identified as 58-year-old actress Cindyana Santangelo, best known for a role in "Married With Children.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Protests against Elon Musk's purge of US government swarm Tesla showrooms
item.description: Crowds have protested billionaire Elon Musk’s purge of the U.S. government under President Donald Trump outside Tesla dealerships throughout the U.S. and in some cities in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Carolina wildfire keeps growing as firefighters protect homes
item.description: A large wildfire in the South Carolina mountains has doubled in size on each of the last three days. But fire crews have been able to keep the blaze away from structures.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boy, 14, dies from apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound in California
item.description: IRVINE (KTLA) — A 14-year-old boy in Irvine has died from an apparent self-inflicted gunshot wound that has resulted in an adult facing criminal charges. Officers and emergency medical personnel responded to a home in the area of Heathergreen and Weepingwood around 2:40 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Son's skeletal remains found in home of man who died in scuba accident days earlier: report
item.description: A Georgia man died in a scuba diving accident merely days before the skeletal remains of his son, who had been missing for four years, were found in his backyard.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell warned Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Officers in Tyre Nichols case back in court ahead of upcoming trial
item.description: Three of the former Memphis Police Officers accused in the beating death of Tyre Nichols are expected in court on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, KRON4

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, Fox 5

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine, the Department of Justice said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Organ transplant patient in Michigan dies from rabies
item.description: A Michigan resident died earlier this year after contracting rabies from an organ transplant, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione may get a laptop in jail to work on his defense in CEO murder case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge cleared the way Thursday for Luigi Mangione to get a laptop behind bars — if his jailers agree — so he can examine documents and other material in the UnitedHealthcare CEO murder case.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
